title: andropogon scoparius var. neomexicanus
intro: Andropogon scoparius var. neomexicanus, also known as broom beard grass or broom grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it a popular choice for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Andropogon scoparius var. neomexicanus include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions.
